steps.title: Use the side ratio
steps.content:
In an isosceles right triangle, if the hypotenuse is $h$, then each leg is $\frac{h}{\sqrt{2}}$. The two legs together have length
$$2\cdot\frac{h}{\sqrt{2}}=h\sqrt{2}$$

steps.title: Match the perimeter
steps.content: The perimeter is $$h+h\sqrt{2}=h(1+\sqrt{2})$$ The given perimeter is $$48+48\sqrt{2}=48(1+\sqrt{2})$$ So $h=48$. The answer is C.
